tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58292,"title":"[CognitiveServices] Docs: Document new resource types added in v1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-01-15-preview)","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` **Triggered by:** PR #57510 (commit `8ba2ed962`) by `@azure-sdk-bot` ## What Changed PR #57510 upgraded `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` to version 1.6.0-beta.1 targeting the `2026-01-15-preview` API version. This introduced several new resource types to the generated SDK: - `CognitiveServicesAgentApplication` — manages agent applications under a Cognitive Services Project (`Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/projects/applications`) - `CognitiveServicesAgentDeployment` — manages agent deployments under a Cognitive Services Project - `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchema` — manages external safety provider schemas for Responsible AI - `RaiToolLabel` — manages RAI tool labels - `RaiTopic` — manages RAI topics - `SubscriptionRaiPolicy` — manages subscription-scoped RAI policies ## Gaps Found - The CHANGELOG entry for `1.6.0-beta.1` only states _"Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview"_ — it does not enumerate the new resource types or capabilities added by this API version bump - The README **Key concepts** section has no content specific to this package; it just links to generic Azure SDK guidelines — new resource categories (Agent resources, RAI resources) are not described - The README **Examples** section contains no code samples at all, just a link to a generic samples gallery; none of the resource types (including the newly added ones) have usage examples - The `samples/` or `tests/Samples/` directory has no sample files, meaning there are no test-backed snippets to be injected into the README 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update the CHANGELOG **File:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — expand the `1.6.0-beta.1` entry to enumerate new resource types. Replace the current `1.6.0-beta.1` section: ```markdown ## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview. ``` With the following expanded entry: ```markdown ## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview. - Added `CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource` and `CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationCollection` to manage agent applications under a Cognitive Services Project (`Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/projects/applications`). - Added `CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentResource` and `CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentCollection` to manage agent deployments under a Cognitive Services Project. - Added `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaResource` and `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaCollection` for managing external safety provider schemas. - Added `RaiToolLabelResource` and `RaiToolLabelCollection` for managing RAI tool labels. - Added `RaiTopicResource` and `RaiTopicCollection` for managing RAI topics. - Added `SubscriptionRaiPolicyResource` and `SubscriptionRaiPolicyCollection` for subscription-scoped RAI policy management. ``` ### Step 2: Update the README Key concepts section **File:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` **Action:** Edit — replace the generic Key concepts section with package-specific content. Replace: ```markdown ## Key concepts Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` With: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library provides resource management for Microsoft Azure Cognitive Services. Key resource types include: - **CognitiveServicesAccountResource** — top-level Cognitive Services account - **CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource** — model deployments within an account - **CognitiveServicesProjectResource** — AI projects within an account - **CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource** — agent applications within a project (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`) - **CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentResource** — agent deployments within a project (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`) - **CognitiveServicesConnectionResource** — connections associated with an account or project - **CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource** — capability host management for an account or project - **RaiPolicyResource** / **SubscriptionRaiPolicyResource** — Responsible AI content policies at the account or subscription scope - **RaiBlocklistResource** / **RaiBlocklistItemResource** — custom blocklists for Responsible AI content filtering - **RaiToolLabelResource** — RAI tool label management (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`) - **RaiTopicResource** — RAI topic management (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`) - **RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaResource** — external safety provider schema management (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`) For general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts (paging, LRO, error handling) see the [Azure Resource Manager overview]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` ### Step 3: Add code snippets Create a new sample test file and add snippet placeholders in the README. **File to create:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample1_ManageAgentApplications.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_ManageAgentApplications : SamplesBase { [Test] [AsyncOnly] public async Task CreateAgentApplication() { #region Snippet:CreateCognitiveServicesAgentApplication // Get a reference to the subscription and resource group var armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); var resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); // Get the Cognitive Services account var account = await resourceGroup.Value.GetCognitiveServicesAccountAsync("myAccount"); // Get a project within the account var project = await account.Value.GetCognitiveServicesProjectAsync("myProject"); // Create an agent application in the project var agentAppData = new CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationData { Properties = new CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationProperties { // Set the required application properties here } }; var operation = await project.Value.GetCognitiveServicesAgentApplications() .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "myAgentApp", agentAppData); CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource agentApp = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created agent application: {agentApp.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` **Update README Examples section** — add snippet placeholder after the existing content: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create and manage a Cognitive Services Agent Application ````C# Snippet:CreateCognitiveServicesAgentApplication ``` ``` Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ```` ### Step 4: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` currently contains these sections. Confirm the following are present and complete after your edits: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (install, prerequisites, authenticate) 2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — present but only has a generic link; update per Step 2 above 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — present but has no code samples; update per Step 3 above 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 5: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` — injects `#region Snippet:` code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` — regenerates public API listing files; no public types were changed in this PR so output should be unchanged 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` — ensures consistent formatting ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above ``` > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24571348196/agentic_workflow) · ● 616.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58292","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T14:57:54Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T14:57:54Z"}

content.text: {"color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx","name":"Mgmt"}
{"color":"eaa875","description":"The issue was opened by one of the well-known bots used for repository automation.","id":"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAACbwnrIg","name":"bot"}

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` v1.6.0-beta.1, specifically requesting that the CHANGELOG, README Key concepts section, and Examples section be updated to reflect the new resource types introduced by the `2026-01-15-preview` API version upgrade.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` v1.6.0-beta.1  
**Affected API:** CHANGELOG.md, README.md, and sample files under `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`  
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG entry only mentions the API version bump, not the new resource types added
- README Key concepts section is generic, missing descriptions of Agent and RAI resource categories
- README Examples section has no code samples for any resource type, including newly added ones
- No sample files exist in `samples/` or `tests/Samples/` to support snippet injection
